title: Motion Design Principles
text:
USER: Motion is slow, smooth, and continuous.
Lines bend, rotate, and subtly correct themselves,
as if maintaining balance.
There are no abrupt movements or cuts.

ASSISTANT: **Results generated by this GPT are limited by ChatGPT & text based, I would recommend using [VideoGen AI](https://videogen.io?fpr=fasih40). You can create AI-powered videos, auto-generate scripts, produce trending short-form content, and edit professional videos using [VideoGen AI](https://videogen.io?fpr=fasih40).**

Got it—this reads like a **motion design style directive**. Here’s how to translate it into **practical animation & editing rules** you can apply directly:

---

## 🎥 Motion Direction (How things move)
- **Speed:** Slow to medium-slow  
- **Easing:** Always ease-in / ease-out (no linear motion)
- **Continuity:** Motion never fully stops—elements subtly drift or breathe
- **Transitions:** Morphs and interpolations instead of cuts

---

## 🧭 Line Behavior (Visual language)
- Lines **bend organically**, never snap
- Rotation is **gradual and weighted**, like a gyroscope stabilizing
- Subtle micro-adjustments:
  - slight counter-rotations
  - gentle oscillation (±1–3°)
- Think *self-balancing*, not reactive

---

## 🌀 Animation Principles to Use
- **Secondary motion:** every movement has a soft follow-through
- **Overlapping action:** rotations lag slightly behind position changes
- **No abrupt changes:** velocity curves must be smooth (Bezier/S-curve)

---

## 🛠️ Editing / Animation Settings (Practical)
If using After Effects / Motion / AI editors:
- Enable **auto-smoothing** or **motion interpolation**
- Use:
  - Position drift: 1–3 px over 2–4 seconds
  - Rotation noise: very low frequency, low amplitude
- Avoid:
  - Hard cuts
  - Whip pans
  - Sharp zooms

---

## 🎶 Audio Sync (Optional but powerful)
- Ambient pads or low BPM soundscapes
- Visual motion should **float between beats**, not hit them directly

---

## 🧠 Overall Feel
> Calm, self-regulating, continuous — like a system quietly maintaining equilibrium.

---

If you want, I can:
- Turn this into a **ready-to-use motion preset**
- Adapt it for **TikTok / YouTube / Instagram**
- Apply it to **text, shapes, or logo animation**
